concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms India's urban landscape. Delhi holds 6.3% of tracked urban population across 7103 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Linear city corridor — Mumbai to Pune
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Mumbai and Pune (120 km apart, combined population 33,204,000). Cities are connected along a corridor averaging 216 km between top pairs — typical of coastal, river valley, or industrial belt development.

narration.synthesis: India shows a distributed urban system with no dominant primate city (Delhi at 6.3%), with gravitational connections forming a corridor (Mumbai → Pune, avg 216 km). Urban power is spread across a geographic axis rather than concentrated at a single hub.
